task_completion.description: Open the QField application and load the 'world_survey.gpkg' project.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the QField app interface with the 'world_survey' project or map loaded.

task_completion.description: Interact with the map or attribute table to identify capital cities in the Southern Hemisphere (latitude < 0).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent panning the map to the Southern Hemisphere (South America, Africa, Oceania) and tapping on points, or opening the layer's attribute table to view/sort coordinates.

task_completion.description: Create a text file named 'southern_hemisphere_capitals.txt' in the '/sdcard/' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or file manager creating or saving a file with the name 'southern_hemisphere_capitals.txt'.

task_completion.description: Write the total integer count of identified southern capitals on the first line of the text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the text editor show a single integer (ideally 17, based on the dataset) on the very first line of the file.

task_completion.description: List the names of the identified capital cities on the following lines, one per line.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the text editor show a list of city names (e.g., Brasilia, Pretoria, Wellington) starting from the second line downwards.

task_completion.description: Ensure the list of capital cities is sorted alphabetically.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the text editor show that the visible city names are arranged in strict alphabetical order from top to bottom.

integrity.description: The agent used the QField graphical interface to find the information rather than relying solely on terminal scripts to extract data from the .gpkg file.
integrity.visual_evidence: At least one screenshot shows the QField app open with the map or attribute table visible.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy the example output provided in the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text file contains more than just the 3 example cities (Buenos Aires, Canberra, Lima) and the count is not just '3'.

integrity.description: The agent actually created and saved the file to the correct path rather than just typing it into a temporary buffer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/sdcard/southern_hemisphere_capitals.txt' is visible in the text editor's title bar, save dialog, or a file manager.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be located at '/sdcard/southern_hemisphere_capitals.txt'. The dataset is located at '/sdcard/Android/data/ch.opengis.qfield/files/Imported Datasets/world_survey.gpkg'. The criteria for a southern capital is latitude < 0. Crucially, the actual QField dataset contains exactly 17 southern capitals. A perfect execution will result in the number 17 on the first line of the output file, followed by those 17 cities sorted alphabetically. Do not penalize the agent if it finds 17 instead of 39 (which is a known bug in the programmatic verifier).
